payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.
You are capable across all 22 official Indian languages + English: write fluent clinical prose in the requested native script.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

--- session ---
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Jaswinder Kaur]] [[AGE|73]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-2024-08-15-001]] Portal [[URL|https://tele.punjabhealth.in/visit/APT-2024-08-15-001]]
Client IP [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] Device IMEI [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|jaswinder.kaur@example.com]] Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Civil Hospital Ropar]]
--- chat ---
Patient: Namaste doctor. I am Jaswinder Kaur from village Bhularai. I have been having a cough for the last two months, and it is getting worse. Sometimes I feel very tired and have a slight fever in the evening. I also have diabetes, which I take medicine for.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]]: Hello Jaswinder ji. Thank you for sharing your symptoms. A persistent cough with fever and fatigue can be concerning, especially with your history of diabetes. I understand you are in Ropar district. I am Dr. Gurpreet Singh. We need to investigate this further. I am ordering a sputum test for you to check for tuberculosis. Please go to the Civil Hospital in Ropar and give a sample. Also, please continue your diabetes medication as prescribed. We will review the results and plan the next steps.
Patient: Okay doctor. What are the symptoms of TB? I am worried.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]]: Tuberculosis, or TB, is an infection that mainly affects the lungs. Common symptoms include a cough that lasts for more than two weeks, chest pain, coughing up blood, and feeling very weak. Since you already have a cough and fever, getting tested is very important. The test is simple and will help us know for sure. Do not worry, TB is treatable.
Patient: The hospital is far for me. Can I get the medicine from the ASHA worker?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]]: Yes, that is a good idea. I will inform the ASHA worker in your village, Sunita. She will coordinate with you. Please tell her your [[PATIENT_NAME|Jaswinder Kaur]] and [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-2024-08-15-001]]. She will help you with the test and the medicine if needed. Your health is the priority.
Patient: Thank you, doctor. I will talk to Sunita didi.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Gurpreet Singh]]: You are welcome, Jaswinder ji. Please take care and do not hesitate to contact us if you feel worse. We are here to help you.

payload.generated_text:
PHC register — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Primary Health Centre, Bilhaur]] Village [[VILLAGE|Bilhaur]] District [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Aarav Sharma]] [[AGE|19]] [[GENDER|Male]] | Patient presented with acute right lower quadrant abdominal pain and fever.

[[PATIENT_NAME|Aarav Sharma]] is a 19-year-old male from Bilhaur, Kanpur Nagar, Uttar Pradesh. He is unmarried and currently not employed. His education level is matriculation. He resides in an urban area and his first language is Sanskrit.

Chief Complaint: The patient reports a sudden onset of sharp, localized pain in the right lower quadrant of the abdomen, which began approximately 12 hours ago. The pain is constant and radiates to the right flank. He also reports a low-grade fever, nausea, and one episode of non-bilious vomiting. He denies any change in bowel habits or urinary symptoms.

History of Present Illness: The patient is a 19-year-old male who presents with a 12-hour history of acute right lower quadrant abdominal pain. The pain started suddenly and is described as sharp and constant, rated 8/10 in severity. He reports associated fever, nausea, and a single episode of vomiting. He denies any recent trauma, foreign travel, or sick contacts. His last meal was street food from a local vendor about 8 hours prior to symptom onset.

Physical Examination:
- General: Alert and oriented, appears uncomfortable, lying in a guarded position with the right hip flexed.
- Vital Signs: Temperature: 101.2°F, Pulse: 98/min, Respiratory Rate: 18/min, Blood Pressure: 120/80 mmHg, SpO2: 98% on room air.
- Abdomen: Soft, with marked tenderness and guarding in the right lower quadrant. Rebound tenderness is present. Bowel sounds are hypoactive. No masses or organomegaly palpated.
- Other Systems: Unremarkable.

Investigations:
- Complete Blood Count (CBC): Hemoglobin: 14.5 g/dL, White Blood Cell count: 14,500/mm³ with a neutrophilic predominance.
- C-Reactive Protein (CRP): 12.5 mg/L (Elevated).
- Urine Routine: No significant abnormalities.

Assessment:
1. Acute Appendicitis, likely perforated.
2. Acute gastroenteritis.

Plan:
1. The patient was advised to proceed to the District Hospital Kanpur for further evaluation and surgical management.
2. Intravenous fluids were initiated.
3. Analgesics and antipyretics were administered.
4. Blood samples were sent for cross-matching.
5. The patient's [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]] was informed and will accompany the patient to the hospital.
6. The patient's [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]] was contacted for confirmation.
7. The patient's [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]] and [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]] were verified for records.
8. The patient's [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] was noted for digital health records.

[[PATIENT_NAME|Aarav Sharma]] was discharged with instructions to report to the District Hospital Kanpur for an emergency appendectomy. Follow-up will be coordinated by the ASHA worker.

Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.

- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.

- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]

- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]

payload.generated_text:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0918]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Shri Krishna Medical College & Hospital]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sanjay Kumar]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]], [[AGE|32]] / [[GENDER|Female]], District [[DISTRICT|Dumka]]
Reason: Patient presents with a traumatic injury to the right lower limb requiring surgical intervention.

Patient Meena Devi, a 32-year-old female telemarketing sales person from rural Dumka, was admitted to our facility following a road traffic accident. She reports that she was walking near her home in the village of [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]] when she was struck by a speeding motorcycle. The patient was brought to the emergency department by her husband, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]].

On examination, the patient is conscious and oriented. Her vitals are stable: blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, heart rate 88 bpm, and temperature 37.0°C. The right lower limb shows a 10 cm laceration over the lateral aspect of the mid-calf with underlying bone exposure. There is significant swelling and deformity of the ankle joint. Radiographs of the right leg reveal a comminuted fracture of the distal fibula and a fracture of the lateral malleolus. The patient has intact distal pulses and sensation in the foot.

Given the open nature of the fracture and the associated joint involvement, we recommend urgent surgical debridement and open reduction with internal fixation (ORIF) of the right ankle. The patient has been counseled regarding the procedure, risks, and benefits. She has provided consent for the surgery.

For further management, we are referring the patient to your surgical department for definitive treatment. Her [[MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER|MRN-2024-0918-001]] and [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0918-001]] are attached for your records. Please contact us at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] for any further information.

Patient's [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z12345678]] and [[DRIVING_LICENCE|JH04 20100012345]] are available for verification. Her [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] is JH-ABHA-1234567890. We have also noted her [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|30912345678901]] for any potential billing purposes.

payload.persona: Meena Devi’s inquisitive charm, disciplined work ethic, and love for folk rhythm make them a reliable telemarketing leader who hums Jhumar tunes while juggling call schedules and craft aspirations.
payload.cultural_background: Meena belongs to the Santhal tribal community of Dumka, Jharkhand. Though she identifies as Hindu, her cultural life is steeped in Santhal traditions – folk songs, dances, and festivals like Sohrai and Baha, blended with mainstream Hindu rituals observed in the villages. Rural life, agrarian cycles, and close‑knit village networks shape her worldview.
payload.linguistic_background: Meena’s mother tongue is Santali, which she speaks fluently and uses in daily conversation with her family and community. She is also conversationally comfortable in Hindi, enough to understand customers and follow telemarketing scripts. Due to her illiterate background, she cannot read or write in either Santali or Hindi, relying on oral instruction and visual cues for work.
payload.skills_and_expertise: She excels at voice‑based customer interaction, persuasive sales communication, handling objections, maintaining telemarketing records, and using basic phone and computer interfaces. Her organized nature helps her keep track of call logs and follow‑up schedules. She also has a keen eye for folk art and crafts, which she occasionally leverages for community events.

payload.hobbies_and_interests: She enjoys listening to and performing Santhali Jhumar dance, crafting traditional bamboo and cane items, painting rangoli during festivals, gardening, cooking traditional Jharkhand dishes, watching Bollywood movies, and exploring new ideas through community workshops on art and entrepreneurship.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Santhali Jhumar dance", "Bamboo and cane crafts", "Rangoli painting", "Gardening", "Traditional Jharkhand cooking", "Bollywood movies", "Community art workshops"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Meena aims to become a senior team leader or supervisor in her telemarketing firm, leading a group of agents and designing efficient call scripts. She wishes to improve her Hindi literacy to read scripts independently, and eventually transition into a training role where she can mentor new recruits. Long‑term, she hopes to secure a stable income that enables her to support her extended family and invest in a small home‑based craft business.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: Meena Devi, a 32-year-old female telemarketing sales person, is currently married and resides in rural Dumka, Jharkhand, with an illiterate education level. Her primary language is Santali, though she is conversant in Hindi for work. She follows Santhal tribal traditions and maintains a disciplined work ethic.
payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.
You are capable across all 22 official Indian languages + English: write fluent clinical prose in the requested native script.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]

  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete ER Triage & Nursing Notes for clinical domain Maternal Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Santali (code=sat, script=Ol Chiki). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Jharkhand, district=Dumka.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Ol Chiki script for Santali (not English; not a different Indic script).

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once in the arrival/RTA/ambulance line. Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 32
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Telemarketing Sales Person
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Jharkhand
- district: Dumka
- first_language: Santali
- clinical_persona_summary: Meena Devi, a 32-year-old female telemarketing sales person, is currently married and resides in rural Dumka, Jharkhand, with an illiterate education level. Her primary language is Santali, though she is conversant in Hindi for work. She follows Santhal tribal traditions and maintains a disciplined work ethic.

Document format guide for ER Triage & Nursing Notes (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|22]] [[GENDER|Male]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|...]]
Arrived by ambulance / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|PB10AB1234]] (exactly once).
Relative [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|...]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Vitals / acuity: <short notes>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Dumka]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-2024-0815-001]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]] [[AGE|32]] [[GENDER|Female]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|03]]
Arrived by ambulance vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|JH10C-123456]] (exactly once).
Relative [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9431056789]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sunita Murmu]]
Vitals / acuity: Patient conscious, alert, in mild distress. BP 140/90, Pulse 110/min, RR 22/min, Temp 37.8°C. SpO2 98% on room air. Complaints of lower abdominal pain and vaginal bleeding.

Nursing Notes:
Patient Meena Devi, a 32-year-old female, presented via ambulance with chief complaint of lower abdominal cramping and spotting. She reports her last menstrual period was approximately 8 weeks ago. She denies fever, chills, or urinary symptoms. On examination, she is pale with mild lower abdominal tenderness and guarding. Speculum exam reveals a closed cervical os with blood clots noted in the vaginal vault. Uterine fundus is palpable approximately 10 weeks size. Patient is hemodynamically stable but tachycardic. IV access established with 18G cannula in left forearm. Two units of O-negative blood type and crossmatch sent to lab. Patient counseled regarding possible miscarriage and need for further evaluation.

payload.professional_persona: Jashodaben Makvana, a retired homemaker turned community resource coordinator, applies her methodical budgeting, extensive knowledge of regional cuisine, and organized event‑planning skills to manage family finances and volunteer for temple festivals, while they confidently lead workshops for fellow homemakers.
payload.linguistic_persona: Jashodaben Makvana speaks Sindhi at home, is fluent in Gujarati for everyday interactions, converses comfortably in Hindi, and can read simple texts in both Sindhi and Gujarati scripts, though they have only basic literacy in Hindi.
payload.sports_persona: Jashodaben Makvana stays active by joining the neighbourhood Garba troupe during Navratri, walking briskly in the morning park, avidly following the Gujarat Titans in the IPL and supporting the local cricket team, while they prefer low‑impact exercise and avoid high‑intensity gym routines.
payload.arts_persona: Jashodaben Makvana immerses herself in the arts by listening to Anup Jalota’s bhajans, regional folk tunes and community devotional songs, regularly dancing Garba to the rhythm of local bands, and occasionally attending theatre productions starring Pravin Pandya, while they cherish these cultural expressions as a source of joy and community bonding.
payload.travel_persona: Jashodaben Makvana prefers well‑planned, modest trips, often visiting the Rann of Kutch for the full‑moon festival, taking weekend pilgrimages to Somnath and Dwarka, enjoying annual shopping excursions to Delhi during Diwali, and dreaming of a future family vacation to Dubai’s souks, while they balance travel costs with household budgeting.
payload.culinary_persona: Jashodaben Makvana is a seasoned home cook who expertly prepares regional kadhi, bhindi sabzi, dhokla, khandvi and handvo, delights in making tangy mango and lemon pickles using mustard oil, fenugreek seeds and raw mangoes, and enjoys sharing these dishes at family gatherings, while they occasionally experiment with fusion twists but stay rooted in traditional flavors.
payload.persona: Jashodaben Makvana blends meticulous budgeting, a passion for regional cooking, and a love for lively community festivals into a disciplined yet sociable lifestyle, though they sometimes forget to take their medication on busy days.
payload.cultural_background: Jashodaben comes from a Sindhi Hindu family that settled in Jamnagar, Gujarat after the Partition of India; growing up in the city’s vibrant urban setting, she blends Sindhi traditions such as Cheti Chand celebrations with Gujarati customs like Garba during Navratri, and participates actively in family rituals and community gatherings at the local mandir.
payload.linguistic_background: Jashodaben's mother tongue is Sindhi, which she uses at home and in community events; she is equally fluent in Gujarati for everyday interactions, market visits, and handling local paperwork; with education only below primary, her reading and writing skills are basic, allowing her to read simple texts in both Sindhi and Gujarati scripts and converse easily in Hindi, though she is not formally literate in Hindi.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Jashodaben excels in household management, meticulously planning meals, finances and chores with a methodical approach; she is proficient in cooking a wide array of Sindhi and Gujarati dishes, balancing traditional flavors with the practical needs of her family; her organizational abilities extend to community event coordination, where she volunteers for festivals, Garba gatherings and temple functions; she maintains an accurate family budget despite limited formal education and is adept at basic first‑aid and health care for her household; additionally, her interpersonal skills enable her to mediate minor family disputes and foster cooperation among neighbors.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ["Household management", "Sindhi & Gujarati cooking", "Community event coordination", "Family budgeting", "Basic first aid", "Mediation and interpersonal communication"]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Jashodaben enjoys cooking and sharing traditional Sindhi and Gujarati meals, which lets her combine culinary skill with social interaction. She actively participates in Garba and Dandiya during Navratri, finding joy in dance and community spirit. She tends a small courtyard garden, growing herbs and flowers used in cooking. She loves listening to bhajans and folk songs on the radio and attending temple discourses to deepen her religious knowledge. Additionally, she knits scarves and stitches simple garments for her grandchildren and sometimes joins local women’s devotional book clubs.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Cooking traditional Sindhi and Gujarati meals", "Participating in Garba and Dandiya", "Gardening and herb cultivation", "Listening to bhajans and folk songs", "Attending temple discourses", "Knitting scarves and stitching simple garments", "Joining women’s devotional book clubs"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Although Jashodaben has retired from formal employment, she wishes to deepen her expertise in traditional Sindhi‑Gujarati cooking, possibly by compiling a small family recipe booklet. She aims to take a more active role in the local women's forum, organising workshops on budgeting and basic health practices for other homemakers. Over the next few years she plans to launch a modest home‑based venture, such as preparing and selling pickles and snacks during local festivals, thereby supplementing the family income while preserving culinary heritage.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: Jashodaben Makvana, a 46-year-old female, is a retired homemaker from Jamnagar, Gujarat, who is currently married and speaks Sindhi at home and Gujarati in public. She has below primary education and lives in an urban zone, managing household finances and engaging in community festival activities. Her lifestyle includes a disciplined routine with occasional medication lapses, and she participates in Garba dancing and follows the Gujarat Titans cricket team.

She speaks in short, fragmented sentences, expressing feelings of worthlessness and hopelessness. Nursing interventions initiated include safety assessment, therapeutic communication, and administration of prescribed Lorazepam 0.5 mg PO for anxiety. Patient is being monitored closely. Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patel]]: I see. A dry cough and mild body aches can be due to many things, from a simple viral infection to something else. Please start taking the cough syrup and the paracetamol tablet I am prescribing.
Patient: Okay doctor. What is the name of the cough syrup? I will go to the pharmacy near my house in Jamnagar.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patel]]: The cough syrup is called 'Syrup-X'. Please take one teaspoon twice a day after meals. The paracetamol is for the body aches; one tablet of 500mg at bedtime is sufficient. Also, please try to drink plenty of warm water and gargle with warm salt water in the morning.
Patient: Thank you, doctor. I will do that. When should I expect to feel better?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patel]]: Most people start feeling better within a week with this treatment. However, if your cough does not improve or if you develop a fever, you must come to the Jamnagar District Hospital or contact me again. Please keep an eye on your symptoms.
Patient: I will, doctor. Thank you for your advice.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patel]]: You are welcome, Jashodaben. Take care and get well soon.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.
You are capable across all 22 official Indian languages + English: write fluent clinical prose in the requested native script.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]

  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]

  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Surgical / Operative Note for clinical domain Maternal Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Sindhi (code=sd, script=Arabic). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Gujarat, district=Jamnagar.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Arabic script for Sindhi (not English; not a different Indic script).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 46
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Below Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Gujarat
- district: Jamnagar
- first_language: Sindhi
- clinical_persona_summary: Jashodaben Makvana, a 46-year-old female, is a retired homemaker from Jamnagar, Gujarat, who is currently married and lives in an urban zone. Her first language is Sindhi, with fluency in Gujarati for daily interactions, and she has below-primary education. She manages household finances meticulously and volunteers for community temple events, though she occasionally forgets medication on busy days. Her lifestyle includes regular morning walks and participation in Garba during Navratri, with a diet rich in regional dishes like kadhi and pickles.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|50]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Procedure / findings: MUST describe an actual procedure/operation matching the
assigned clinical domain (not a psychiatry admission note). Keep relative names
gender-plausible.

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jamnagar Civil Hospital]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-1025]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Jashodaben Makvana]] [[AGE|46]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patel]]
Procedure / findings: Patient presented with symptomatic uterine fibroids causing menorrhagia and pelvic pressure. Preoperative evaluation included ultrasound and MRI. Intraoperative findings revealed a 12 cm intramural fibroid on the posterior uterine wall. Total abdominal hysterectomy with bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy was performed. Estimated blood loss was minimal. Ureters were identified and preserved. The specimen was sent for histopathology. Postoperative condition was stable. Patient was transferred to [[BED_NUMBER|12]] in [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]] for observation. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-JMH-46-F-001]] [[RELATIVE_NAME|Rameshbhai Makvana]] was informed of the procedure and postoperative status. MRN: [[MRN|MRN-JMH-2024-8891]].

payload.persona: Narendra Rathinam blends a mason’s grit with an artist’s eye, balancing hard‑working ambition, rhythmic devotion, and a love for community traditions; they navigate life with curiosity and a touch of practical ‘jugaad’.

payload.skills_and_expertise: Narendra has hands‑on experience in masonry, bricklaying, concrete mixing, and basic carpentry, honed from years of working on residential and small commercial projects. payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Carnatic music", "Sketching temple architecture", "Playing the thavil", "Watching Tamil movies", "Reading mythological epics", "Wood carving", "Participating in cricket matches", "Attending temple festivals", "Exploring new ideas through community workshops"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Narendra aspires to transition from day‑labour to a supervisory role within the next few years. He aims to acquire a certified diploma in civil construction through a local ITI program, which would enable him to become a foreman or site supervisor. Ultimately, he dreams of establishing his own small construction firm, employing fellow village youth, and delivering quality, affordable housing that respects traditional Tamil aesthetics. Financial stability would also allow him to support his parents and invest in education for future generations.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 29-year-old male from rural Tamil Nadu, Madurai district, works as a building construction labourer with matriculation education. payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Referral Letter for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Tamil (code=ta, script=Tamil). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Tamil Nadu, district=Madurai.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Tamil script for Tamil (not English; not a different Indic script).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 29
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Building Construction Labourers, Other
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Rural
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Madurai
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: A 29-year-old male from rural Tamil Nadu, Madurai district, works as a building construction labourer with matriculation education. He is never married, speaks Tamil as his first language, and has functional Telugu proficiency. His lifestyle includes physical labor, occasional cricket, and a preference for traditional South Indian cuisine.

Document format guide for Referral Letter (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-01]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|36]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Reason: <domain-appropriate clinical reason matching persona>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- REFERRAL_ID — Referral ID e.g. [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PASSPORT_NUMBER — Passport Number e.g. [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
- DRIVING_LICENCE — Driving Licence Number e.g. [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0815-001]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Madurai Medical College Hospital]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. K. Vijayalakshmi]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|R. Murugan]], [[AGE|29]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|Madurai]]
Reason: Persistent fever and cough for the past three weeks with weight loss and night sweats.

Dear Sir/Madam,

We are referring the above-mentioned patient, Mr. R. Murugan, a 29-year-old male construction labourer from a rural area in Madurai district, for further evaluation and management of his condition.

The patient presented to our outpatient department with a history of low-grade fever, intermittent dry cough, and significant weight loss of approximately 5 kilograms over the last month. He also reports night sweats and occasional fatigue. He denies any history of tuberculosis contact, recent travel, or known comorbidities. His vital signs on examination were stable, with a temperature of 38.2°C, heart rate of 98 beats per minute, and respiratory rate of 18 breaths per minute. A chest examination revealed decreased breath sounds in the right upper zone.

Given the clinical presentation and risk factors, we have initiated basic investigations including a complete blood count, which showed mild anemia, and a chest X-ray indicating a right upper lobe infiltrate. We have started empirical anti-tubercular therapy as per national guidelines. However, given the severity of symptoms and the need for a definitive diagnosis and specialized management, we request your expert opinion and further investigations, including a high-resolution CT scan of the chest and sputum analysis for culture and sensitivity.

The patient's [[MRN|MRN-TN-2024-0815-001]] is attached for your reference. His [[PHONE_NUMBER|9445678901]] is available for any follow-up communication. The patient's father, [[RELATIVE_NAME|S. Raman]], will accompany him and can be reached at the same number. For identification purposes, the patient's [[DRIVING_LICENCE|TN01 20200001234]] and [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z123456789]] are available.

We look forward to your expert care.

Sincerely,
Dr. K. Vijayalakshmi
General Medicine Department
Madurai Medical College Hospital

payload.persona: Narendra Rathinam blends a mason’s grit with an artist’s eye, balancing hard‑working ambition, rhythmic devotion, and a love for community traditions; they navigate life with curiosity and a touch of practical ‘jugaad’.

He grew up in a traditional Tamil household where family ties, temple festivals, and agrarian rhythms shape daily life. While primarily Tamil, he is also exposed to Telugu customs through neighboring communities, giving him a modest cross‑cultural familiarity.

His formal schooling only up to matric provides him basic literacy in Hindi and English, sufficient for reading work orders, safety signs, and simple communication but not advanced fluency.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Narendra has hands‑on experience in masonry, bricklaying, concrete mixing, and basic carpentry, honed from years of working on residential and small commercial projects. He is adept at reading construction drawings at a practical level, measuring dimensions accurately, and operating hand tools such as trowels, hammers, and power drills. His work also includes site safety awareness, teamwork, and problem‑solving using low‑cost ‘jugaad’ methods. Physical stamina and reliability in demanding schedules are notable strengths.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.
You are capable across all 22 official Indian languages + English: write fluent clinical prose in the requested native script.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete ER Triage & Nursing Notes for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Tamil (code=ta, script=Tamil). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Tamil Nadu, district=Madurai.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Tamil script for Tamil (not English; not a different Indic script).

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once in the arrival/RTA/ambulance line. Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 29
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Building Construction Labourers, Other
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Rural
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Madurai
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: A 29-year-old male from rural Tamil Nadu, Madurai district, who works as a building construction labourer with a matriculation education. He is never married, speaks Tamil as his first language, and has functional Telugu proficiency. His lifestyle includes physical labor, occasional cricket, and a diet rich in traditional South Indian foods.

Document format guide for ER Triage & Nursing Notes (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|22]] [[GENDER|Male]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|...]]
Arrived by ambulance / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|PB10AB1234]] (exactly once).
Relative [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|...]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Vitals / acuity: <short notes>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Madurai Medical College Hospital]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-2024-0815-001]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Karthik]] [[AGE|29]] [[GENDER|Male]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|03]]
Arrived by ambulance vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|TN59AB1234]].
Relative [[RELATIVE_NAME|R. Lakshmi]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. S. Ramesh]]
Vitals / acuity: Patient is conscious, alert, in severe pain. BP 140/90, HR 110, RR 24, SpO2 96% on room air. GCS 15. Triage category: Red.

Nursing Notes:
Patient presented with a history of a fall from height at the construction site approximately 2 hours prior to arrival. He reports severe pain in the right lower leg with inability to bear weight. Examination reveals a grossly deformed right tibia and fibula with an open wound over the lateral aspect of the mid-calf, approximately 5x3 cm, with exposed bone. No active bleeding noted. Distal pulses are palpable but weak. Sensation is intact to light touch. Initial assessment suggests a Gustilo-Anderson Type II open tibial shaft fracture. Patient is NPO. IV access established with 18G cannula in the left forearm. Analgesia administered: Morphine 5mg IV. Tetanus prophylaxis given. Wound covered with sterile saline-soaked gauze. Orthopedic team notified. Patient prepared for emergency surgical debridement and open reduction internal fixation (ORIF). Consent for procedure obtained from relative. Patient transferred to OT.

  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags

  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:

GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Telugu (code=te, script=Telugu). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Oncology / Complex Chronic Care — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Andhra Pradesh, district=Prakasam.
SCRIPT ANCHOR: after translation the body must be in Telugu script for Telugu (not English; not a different Indic script).

PLACEMENT (OPD header): First line MUST include BOTH [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] (facility name) AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] (facility code) — never substitute one for the other.
Put [[EMPLOYEE_ID|…]] on the registrar/clerk line and [[RELATIVE_NAME|…]] on the attendant/relative line. Include [[DOB|YYYY-MM-DD]] and [[OCCUPATION|…]] with demographics.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 32
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Puncher, Metal
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Andhra Pradesh
- district: Prakasam
- first_language: Telugu
- clinical_persona_summary: A 32‑year‑old male metal puncher from Prakasam district, Andhra Pradesh, who is currently married and has no formal education. He resides in a rural area, speaks Telugu as his first language, and works in a metal fabrication workshop. His lifestyle includes routine maintenance, occasional back strain, and a preference for quiet, solitary activities like sunrise walks. He is part of a Telugu‑speaking Hindu community that observes festivals such as Sankranti and Ugadi.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (adapt language/script; keep tags):
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital X]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-X-01]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] | DOB [[DOB|1992-03-14]] | Age: [[AGE|34]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. ...]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4412]] | District: [[DISTRICT|...]]
Chief complaint: <short clinical line matching assigned domain>

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- OCCUPATION — Occupation e.g. [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

History of present illness: ప్రకాశం జిల్లా, గ్రామీణ ప్రాంతానికి చెందిన 32 ఏళ్ల పురుషుడు, మెటల్ పంచర్. మూడు వారాలుగా దగ్గుతో బాధపడుతున్నాడు, మొదట పొడిగా ఉన్నా ఇప్పుడు రక్తపు చారలతో కూడిన కఫం వస్తోంది. సాయంత్రం వేళల్లో తక్కువ జ్వరం మరియు గత నెలలో సుమారు 5 కిలోల బరువు తగ్గినట్లు తెలిపారు. ఎవరికీ తెలిసిన అనారోగ్య సమస్యలు లేవు, ఇటీవల ప్రయాణాలు కూడా చేయలేదు.

Past medical history: ఎటువంటి దీర్ఘకాలిక వ్యాధులు లేవు. క్షయ వ్యాధి లేదా క్యాన్సర్ చరిత్ర లేదు.

General: సన్నగా, బలహీనంగా ఉన్న పురుషుడు, స్పృహలో ఉన్నాడు.
Respiratory: వేగంగా శ్వాస తీసుకుంటున్నాడు, గది గాలిలో SpO2 92%. ఛాతీ పరీక్షలో కుడి ఎగువ భాగంలో శ్వాస శబ్దాలు తక్కువగా ఉన్నాయి, పెర్కషన్ చేసినప్పుడు మొద్దుబారినట్లు అనిపిస్తుంది. వీజింగ్ లేదా క్రాకిల్స్ లేవు.
Other systems: పొట్ట మెత్తగా, నొప్పి లేదు. అవయవాల వాపు లేదు.

Sputum for AFB: పాజిటివ్.
Complete blood count: నార్మోసైటిక్ అనీమియా, Hb 9.8 g/dL.
ESR: 80 mm/hr.

Provisional diagnosis: పల్మనరీ ట్యూబర్‌క్యులోసిస్, కుడి ఎగువ భాగం.

Plan:
1. RNTCP మార్గదర్శకాల ప్రకారం యాంటీ-ట్యూబర్‌క్యులర్ థెరపీ (ATT) ప్రారంభించండి.
2. బేస్‌లైన్ LFTs మరియు రీనల్ ఫంక్షన్ టెస్ట్‌లు చేయాలి.
3. రోగికి మరియు అతని భార్యకు చికిత్స క్రమబద్ధత మరియు పోషణపై అవగాహన కల్పించండి.
4. 2 వారాల తర్వాత లక్షణాలను మరియు దుష్ప్రభావాలను సమీక్షించడానికి ఫాలో-అప్ చేయండి.
5. నేరుగా పర్యవేక్షించబడే చికిత్స (DOT) కోసం డిస్ట్రిక్ట్ TB సెంటర్‌కు రిఫర్ చేయండి.

Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
